mechanism: Rising term premium can trigger basis trade unwinds: as Treasury prices fall, levered basis trade positions (50-100x) face margin calls, forcing Treasury selling that further increases term premium, creating a doom loop. The basis trade converts term premium shocks from a smooth repricing into a non-linear liquidity event.
regime_dependence: Active when basis trade positions are large ($800B-$1T) and leveraged. Supply-driven term premium increases (fiscal expansion) are the most dangerous trigger because they lack the growth offset that would support risk assets.
